title: Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices 13 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Birth Parents Disability Benefit Recipients Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients Former Foster Children Foster Children Foster Families Medicaid Recipients Parents of Children in Foster Care TANF Recipients 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details CITY OF AMITY COMPLAINT FORM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community members a service to report experiencing or witnessing a nuisance such as public nuisance, discourteous or unresponsive city employee, obstruction of a city street or sidewalk, illegal parking, trailer or RV being used as a residence, possible unpermitted construction activity, or other suspected city code violations. 109 Maddox Avenue Amity, OR 97101 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (503) 835-3711 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF EUGENE CITY OF EUGENE OFFICE OF EQUITY & COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT HUMAN RIGHTS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Fields discrimination complaints related to housing, employment, public access (including accessibility requests and grievances), and city contracts that occur within the City of Eugene. Also provides victim and community information and referrals in response to hate and bias activity. 500 East 4th Avenue Suite 101 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday by appointment only Email (541) 682-5177 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND INDEPENDENT POLICE REVIEW DIVISION INDEPENDENT POLICE REVIEW DIVISION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts commendations from the public for good police work, as well as accepting and investigating complaints of Portland Police Bureau (PPB) misconduct. Independent Police Review division offers civilian oversight of Portland Police Bureau, monitors internal affairs investigations, and recommends policy changes to City Council and Chief of Police. 1221 SW 4th Avenue Room 140 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm In person: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (503) 823-4571 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND OFFICE OF THE OMBUDSMAN PORTLAND OFFICE OF THE OMBUDSMAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Receives complaints from the public about city government agencies. The Ombudsman is an independent office that conducts impartial investigations and recommends solutions. 1221 SW 4th Avenue Room 310 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 823-0144 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONSUMER FINANCIAL PROTECTION BUREAU FINANCIAL PRODUCTS COMPLAINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps consumers connect with financial companies to understand issues, fix errors, and get direct responses about problems. 1700 G Street NW Washington, DC 20552 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 6am-3pm except federal holidays (855) 411-2372 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OFFICE OF OREGON GOVERNOR GOVERNMENT COMPLAINTS AND OMBUDSMAN OFFICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a list of Oregon ombudsman offices including Foster Care Ombudsman, Long-Term Care Ombudsman, Oregon Public Guardian and Conservator Program (OPG), Residential Facilities Ombudsman, Governor's Advocacy Office (ODHS), Office of Small Business Assistance, Small Business Ombudsman for Workers’ Compensation, Ombuds Office for Oregon Workers, Oregon Health Authority (OHA) Ombuds Program, and Office of Corrections Ombudsman. 775 Court Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 378-4582 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE SANCTUARY PROMISE HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tracks and reports sanctuary promise violations. Provides victim and witness support, information, and a referral hotline. The Oregon Department of Justice (ODOJ) may open an investigation into reported sanctuary promise law violations. Hotline advocates do not control the outcome of ODOJ investigations or decisions to sanction a perpetrator of a violation. 1162 Court Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (884) 924-7829 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON OCCUPATIONAL SAFETY AND HEALTH DIVISION OMBUDSMAN FOR INJURED WORKERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with informally resolving any problems or disputes that may arise between the parties involved in a claim, and can answer questions about the workers' compensation system. Office was created by the Oregon Legislature to be an independent advocate for Oregon ́s injured workers. Cannot provide legal advice and will not represent a person at Workers ́ Compensation Board hearings. 350 Winter Street NE 3rd Floor Salem, OR 97309 Eligibility: Anyone involved with a workplace injury Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 378-3351 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to objectively evaluate and work to resolve issues and concerns presented by individuals who contact the Governor's Advocacy Office. These concerns include, but are not limited to: -Child abuse and neglect -Adult and elder abuse or neglect -Access to ODHS public assistance programs -Customer service matters -Resources for individual and family support services or treatment programs, including services for individuals with physical, mental or developmental disabilities. 500 Summer Street NE E-17 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (800) 442-5238 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE FOSTER CARE OMBUDSMAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves as an independent resource to investigate complaints, concerns, or violations of rights for children in the custody of ODHS Foster Care. Established through legislation developed and passed by current and former foster youth, the office receives, investigates, and helps resolve concerns from a wide range of people, including foster youth, parents, relatives, CASAs, attorneys, social workers, and other interested parties. The Ombudsman listens to concerns, documents complaints, gathers all relevant information, remains neutral and impartial, and provides guidance on how to help. Operates the Youth Empowerment and Safety (YES) line, a hotline available not only for complaints but for foster youth to ask questions about their rights, care and well-being, or any issues with their placement or services while in care. 500 Summer Street NE E-17 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (855) 840-6036 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS OHA OMBUDS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Advocates for individuals by investigating and resolving complaints related to the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and the public option plan. Provides support with enrollment issues and helps Oregonians access health care providers. Can also assist small businesses 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (877) 642-0450 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about enforcement of laws against discrimination based on race, color, national origin, disability, age, religion, and sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity) by health care and human services. Also provides information, enforcement, and a complaint process for HIPPA for individuals and professionals. 200 Independence Avenue SW Washington, DC 20201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 368-1019 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF STATE OFFICE OF INSPECTOR GENERAL HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Fields allegations regarding fraud, waste, abuse, mismanagement, or misconduct affecting Department of State programs and operations. 2201 C Street NW Washington, DC 20037 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (202) 647-3320 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF AMITY COMPLAINT FORM CITY OF EUGENE HUMAN RIGHTS PROGRAM CITY OF PORTLAND INDEPENDENT POLICE REVIEW DIVISION CITY OF PORTLAND PORTLAND OFFICE OF THE OMBUDSMAN OFFICE OF OREGON GOVERNOR GOVERNMENT COMPLAINTS AND OMBUDSMAN OFFICES OREGON DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE SANCTUARY PROMISE HOTLINE OREGON OCCUPATIONAL SAFETY AND HEALTH DIVISION OMBUDSMAN FOR INJURED WORKERS OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES GOVERNOR'S ADVOCACY OFFICE OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS OHA OMBUDS PROGRAM
